channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The next wave of AI is here: Autonomous AI agents are amazing—and scary
item.description: The relentless hype around AI makes it difficult to separate the signal from the noise. So it’s understandable if you’ve tuned out recent talk about autonomous AI agents. A word of advice: Don’t. The significance of agentic AI may actually exceed the hype.
item.dc:creator: Tom Barnett, Fast Company

item.title: This is the weirdest laptop I've ever seen and it reminds me of an often-mocked, thoroughly misunderstood Toshiba notebook
item.description: The Bapaco is a a Windows 11 laptop that's also a display and keyboard for Windows, macOS and Android.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Apple drops encryption feature for UK users after government reportedly demanded backdoor access
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Apple said Friday it will stop offering an advanced data security option for British users after the government reportedly demanded that the company provide backdoor access for any data those users have stored in the cloud.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Q&A: East Bay AI safety expert talks about the future of the technology
item.description: Artificial intelligence safety expert and Redwood Research CEO Buck Shlegeris explains the different philosophies guiding A.I. advancement.
item.dc:creator: Chase Hunter, The Mercury News

item.title: Optimization is a biproduct of Army’s network consolidation
item.description: Leo Garciga, the Army’s CIO, said the Unified Network Optimization initiative is helping the service cut costs and duplicative applications. The post Optimization is a biproduct of Army’s network consolidation first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: DOJ dropping immigration case against Musk's SpaceX
item.description: The Department of Justice (DOJ) said Thursday it is planning to drop its lawsuit against Elon Musk's SpaceX, which alleged the company discriminated against refugees and asylees during the hiring process.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: A cheaper YouTube Premium Lite tier could roll out soon – and as a Spotify fan I'm ready to sign up
item.description: A new, cheaper YouTube Premium tier could launch soon, and it's perfect for those who just want videos and not YouTube Music.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: Chaos ahead: emerging technologies will test data privacy, says Britain’s ICO
item.description: Emerging technologies such as connected transport, quantum sensing, healthcare diagnostics, and synthetic AI-driven content are creating huge gaps in data protection and privacy that could quickly outpace the ability of regulators and enterprises to contain them.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Charred EV batteries from California wildfires spark concern about toxic waste contaminating drinking water
item.description: A water agency in charge of ground water for 2 million L.A. County residents has several concerns about hazardous waste processing from the Eaton fire.
item.dc:creator: Steve Scauzillo, The Mercury News

item.title: Defense tech company True Anomaly expands into California
item.description: True Anomaly, a defense technology company, announced its expansion into Southern California with a 90,000-square-foot factory in Long Beach.
item.dc:creator: Christina Merino, The Mercury News

item.title: Google’s new AI co-scientist aims to speed up the scientific discovery process
item.description: Google has unveiled an AI co-scientist built on its Gemini 2.0 platform, aiming to accelerate scientific research by generating novel hypotheses and refining experiments. While the system shows promise, questions remain about its performance and broader applicability.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Playing by the rules of AI
item.description: The Fast Company Impact Council is a private membership community of influential leaders, experts, executives, and entrepreneurs who share their insights with our audience. Members pay annual membership dues for access to peer learning and thought leadership opportunities, events and more.
item.dc:creator: Juan Orlandini, Fast Company

item.title: iPhones may soon be able to use satellite connections indoors
item.description: Sending messages via satellite on iPhones should soon be as easy to as using your mobile network signal, as Apple and satellite partner Globalstar put a new satellite constellation into space.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Nvidia: Latest news and insights
item.description: With its legacy of innovation in GPU technology, Nvidia has become a dominant force in the AI market. Nvidia’s partners read like a technology who’s who list – e.g.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: 3 reasons to consider a data security posture management platform
item.description: A week rarely goes by without a major data security breach. Recent news includes a breach impacting an energy company’s 8 million customers, another compromising the information on 450,000 current and former students, and one more exposing 240,000 credit union members.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: A top Chinese official tours Thai-Myanmar border to highlight crackdown on scam centers
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Efforts to shut down online scam centers in Myanmar appeared to gain momentum on Monday as a top Chinese security official visited both sides of the Thai-Myanmar border ahead of expected large-scale repatriations of workers in the illicit industry.
item.dc:creator: JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: What changes to the CHIPS act could mean for AI growth and consumers
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Even as he's vowed to push the United States ahead in artificial intelligence research, President Donald Trump's threats to alter federal government contracts with chipmakers and slap new tariffs on the semiconductor industry may put new speed bumps in front of the tech industry.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Inflatable tiny house with space for six is blowing up
item.description: An inflatable six-person tiny house from a firm called 2001 looks like a space station with its inflatable dome that is transportable and eco-friendly.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
